en: Mmm, so good. I want a cheeseburger.
ko: 음, 정말 맛있어. 치즈버거 먹고 싶어.

en: This is a special soup called pho. You told me not to say that word.
It is delicious. This is the food of your people.
Can I get you anything else? Actually, we're just trying to teach our daughter about your country.
And as I'm saying that, I'm hoping you're from Vietnam. I was born there.
ko: 내가 그렇게 말하면서, 네가 베트남 출신이길 바라고 있어. 나는 거기서 태어났어. 릴리도 그래.

en: So was Lily. Oh, it's a beautiful country.
ko: 아, 정말 아름다운 나라야. 내 가족은 아직도 거기 살아.

en: My family still lives there. I hate Vietnam.
Lily, honey, we don't hate. I hate Vietnam. Mom.
OK, we need just a second. Lily, that was rude.
I want to go home. No, it's important that you celebrate the culture of your ancestors.
You are Vietnamese. No, I'm not. I'm gay. I'm gay.
ko: 아니, 나는 아니야. 나는 게이야. 나는 게이야. 자기야, 아니야, 너는 게이가 아니야. 너는 그냥 혼란스러운 거야.

en: Honey, no, you're not gay. You are just confused.
Oh, my god, what is wrong with me? It's like I'm, oh, please, we have tons of lesbian friends.
Well, I thought you would reference our friends and not us. I'm so gay. We should just go.
ko: 그녀가 수프를 다 먹을 때까지. 아니, 글로리아, 괜찮아.

en: No, nobody's going anywhere until she finishes the whole soup. Don't worry, it's fine.
No, no, no, it's not fine. That's the problem with this country.
Nobody cares where they come from. They just want cheeseburgers and prep school,
ko: 아무도 자신이 어디서 왔는지 신경 쓰지 않아.
